LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF NHAN PHAN'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF MAY FILE HER PROPOSED THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT ("TAC") WITHIN 10 DAYS OF ENTRY OF THIS ORDER. COURTS ARE BOUND TO APPLY A GREAT LIBERALITY IN PERMITTING AMENDMENTS TO THE COMPLAINT ABSENT PREJUDICE TO THE ADVERSE PARTY. (ATKINSON V. ELK CORP. (2003) 109 CAL.APP.4TH 739, 761.) DEFENDANTS HAVE NOT ESTABLISHED THAT THEY WOULD BE PREJUDICED BY THE FILING OF THE PROPOSED TAC. NO TRIAL DATE HAS BEEN SET AND THE FACTUAL ALLEGATIONS REMAIN LARGELY THE SAME BETWEEN THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT AND THE PROPOSED TAC. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976 (302/MJM)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Aug-15-2016 Vacated

MASTER CALENDAR JURY

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-09-2015 Set For Trial On May-31-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

OBJECTION HEARING CALENDAR
Off Calendar Nov-25-2015 Off Calendar

DEMURRER TO 4TH AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-07-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. JOHNATHON LA, ESQ. of Tsao-Wu, Chow & Yee LLP, Tel No. 415-777-1688 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. GEOFFREY ROTWEIN, ESQ. Tel No. 415-397-0860 appearing for Defendant Oanh Thanh.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT OANH THANH'S DEMURRER TO 4TH AMENDED COMPLAINT is overruled in its entirety. The fact that plaintiff added a second cause of action for breach of oral contract as to Defendants Dang and Nhung, a third cause of action for breach of oral contract as to Defendants Nguyen and Thanh, and specified Defendant Dang in the first cause of action for breach of written contract does not provide grounds for sustaining Defendant's demurrer to the entire complaint. The defendants' participation in forming the alleged contracts is a question of fact that should not be decided on a motion for a demurrer. The remaining grounds for the demurrer are also inappropriate for resolution on a non-evidentiary motion such as a demurrer. All of the claims alleged against defendant Oanh Yen Thi Thanh are sufficiently pled. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/HEK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Fourth Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Oct-07-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. JOHNATHON LA, ESQ. of Tsao-Wu, Chow & Yee LLP, Tel No. 415-777-1688 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. GEOFFREY ROTWEIN, ESQ. Tel No. 415-397-0860 appearing for Defendant Oanh Thanh.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT OANH THANH'S Motion To Strike Fourth Amended Complaint is denied in its entirety. It is at best unclear whether the fourth amended complaint contravenes the letter or spirit of the March 20, 2015 order. There are good reasons to believe that the fourth amended complaint was drafted to conform to that order. To the extent, if any, that the fourth amended complaint contravenes that order by alleging new causes of action or making new substantive allegations, the interests of justice require that the plaintiff be permitted to do so and there is no legal prejudice to defendant Oanh Yen Thi Thanh. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/HEK)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-21-2015 Continued To Nov-25-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order That Genuineness Of Documents Be Deemed Admitted And For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $1860.00
Off Calendar Apr-22-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Paul Horcher, Esq. of the Law Office of Paul Horcher, Tel. 415.385.2992, for defendants and moving party;
No other appearances at the hearing.

RULING: Defendants' counsel stipulated to the Judge Pro Tem. There being no objection, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on DEFENDANT MICHAEL DANG, DBA AS BLUE SKY SPA's Motion For An Order That Genuineness Of Documents Be Deemed Admitted And For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $1860.00 as follows: The Motion for an Order that the Genuineness of Documents Be Deemed Admitted and for Sanctions is DENIED. Where responses have been timely filed but are deemed deficient by the requesting party (e.g., because of objections or evasive responses), that party may move for an order compelling a further response, not to have the request for genuineness deemed admitted. CCP 2033.290. The Court treats the Motion as a motion to compel further responses to the RFAS. On that basis, the Motion is GRANTED. Defendant Nhan Thi Phan shall serve complete and straightforward verified responses to each request for genuineness within two weeks of the date of this order. The request for sanctions is DENIED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge Pro Tem: Peter Catalanotti; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Not reported.

(After the matter was called, Shirlney Tung, Esq. of Tsao-Wu Chow & Yee LLP, showed up and was given a copy of the order by this clerk.)

Off Calendar Mar-20-2015 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT OANH THANH's DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT is Sustained with 10 days leave to amend. The Third Amended Complaint ("TAC") is uncertain because it does not comply with CRC 2.112(4). Defendant Oanh Yen Thi Thanh cannot reasonably respond to the TAC because it is unclear which of the twelve causes of action are alleged against her. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Paula Shi, CSR # 13358 =302/eg

Moving party must deliver courtesy copies of the moving papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than February 20, 2015 at 4:00 pm or the motion will be denied. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The court has courtesy copies of the opposition papers. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Sean Kane, Not Reported. (302/EHG)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Third Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Aug-13-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. JONATHAN LA OF THE TSAO-WU, CHOW & YEE LLP, TEL. NO. 777-1688 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF NHAN PHAN's Motion For Leave To File Third Amended Complaint is Granted. Plaintiff may file her proposed Third Amended Complaint ("TAC") within 10 days of entry of this Order. Courts are bound to apply a great liberality in permitting amendments to the complaint absent prejudice to the adverse party. (Atkinson v. Elk Corp. (2003) 109 Cal.App.4th 739, 761.) Defendants have not established that they would be prejudiced by the filing of the proposed TAC. No trial date has been set and the factual allegations remain largely the same between the Second Amended Complaint and the proposed TAC. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/MJM
